citations.narrative:
During the inspection, the facility representative informed the inspector that 3 emus, several blackucks and a lemur had
been born on the property since the last inspection and a nine-banded armadillo was obtained from the wild. Upon review
of the records, no documentation of the birth, hatching or acquisition of these animals was available. Records of
acquisition and disposition of animals are required for traceback of possible animal disease or to ensure humane
treatment of animals in commerce. Every dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly
disclose the following information, purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, leased, or otherwise in his or her
possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, sold, euthanized, or otherwise disposed of by that dealer
or exhibitor. The records shall include any offspring born or hatched of any animal while in his or her possession or under
his or her control.
Going forward records of all acquisitions and dispositions of animals shall be maintained.
Correct By: 5/6/26

citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
Two bird enclosures were bare and did not contain any environmental enrichment. One enclosure contained a Goffin's
Cockatoo and the other enclosure contained both a Scarlet and Blue and Gold Macaw. Both Cockatoos and Macaws are
very intelligent species and require additional environmental stimulation in their primary enclosures. Not providing
environmental enrichment for these birds could lead to distress and possible self-harm. The physical environment in the
primary enclosures must be enriched by materials or activities that would provide the birds with the means to express
noninjurious species-typical activities.
Environmental Enrichment should be added to both enclosures.
Correct By 5/13/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
Additional Inspectors:
Emily Steele, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
During the inspection, the facility representative informed the inspector that 3 emus, several blackucks and a lemur had 
been born on the property since the last inspection and a nine-banded armadillo was obtained from the wild. Upon review 
of the records, no documentation of the birth, hatching or acquisition of these animals was available. Records of 
acquisition and disposition of animals are required for traceback of possible animal disease or to ensure humane 
treatment of animals in commerce. Every dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly 
disclose the following information, purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, leased, or otherwise in his or her 
possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, sold, euthanized, or otherwise disposed of by that dealer 
or exhibitor. The records shall include any offspring born or hatched of any animal while in his or her possession or under 
his or her control. 
 
Going forward records of all acquisitions and dispositions of animals shall be maintained. 
Correct By: 5/6/26 
3.154(b) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
Two bird enclosures were bare and did not contain any environmental enrichment. One enclosure contained a Goffin's 
Cockatoo and the other enclosure contained both a Scarlet and Blue and Gold Macaw. Both Cockatoos and Macaws are 
very intelligent species and require additional environmental stimulation in their primary enclosures. Not providing 
environmental enrichment for these birds could lead to distress and possible self-harm. The physical environment in the 
primary enclosures must be enriched by materials or activities that would provide the birds with the means to express 
noninjurious species-typical activities.  
 
Environmental Enrichment should be added to both enclosures. 
Correct By 5/13/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.

Additional Inspectors: 
Emily Steele, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
